description:
## Problem

c11 workspaces can already carry a custom color, but individual surface tabs inside panes cannot. Operators can color a workspace in the sidebar and theme chrome around that workspace, but they cannot mark the terminal/browser/markdown surfaces inside a pane with distinct colors. In dense multi-agent workspaces, pane tabs need their own lightweight identity markers so a user can distinguish roles, tasks, or risk levels without renaming every tab.

## Current architecture notes

- Workspace custom color lives on `Workspace.customColor`, mutates through `Workspace.setCustomColor(_:)`, persists as `SessionWorkspaceSnapshot.customColor`, and is exposed over `workspace.set_custom_color` / `workspace.list`.
- Pane tabs are Bonsplit `TabItem`s keyed by surface/panel UUIDs. c11 keeps panel-owned state in `Workspace` dictionaries keyed by panel ID (`panelCustomTitles`, `pinnedPanelIds`, unread state, per-panel git/PR/ports, etc.) and mirrors renderable state into Bonsplit through `BonsplitController.updateTab`.
- Workspace color palette and custom color normalization/display helpers already exist as `WorkspaceTabColorSettings`; despite the name, they currently serve workspace colors.
- Surface metadata is a separate manifest contract for agents/Lattice. It may expose tab color later, but tab color should be a first-class c11 UI preference, not only opaque metadata.

## Desired direction

Add per-surface/pane-tab custom colors. A tab color belongs to the surface/panel identity and follows that surface when it is reordered, moved between panes, or detached/attached across workspaces/windows. It should render in the Bonsplit tab strip without changing Ghostty terminal content, browser page content, or the workspace-level custom color.

## Scope

- Add a nullable custom color field for individual surface tabs, normalized as `#RRGGBB`.
- Persist tab colors in session snapshots and restore them across app restart.
- Preserve tab colors across same-workspace moves, cross-pane moves, detach/attach, and cross-workspace/window surface moves.
- Add tab context-menu affordances for setting, choosing custom, and clearing a tab color.
- Reuse or deliberately rename/generalize the existing workspace color palette/settings helper so workspace and tab color UX share a palette without confusing code or user-facing copy.
- Render a compact color indicator in the Bonsplit tab strip for selected and unselected tabs, including small/narrow tab widths.
- Expose tab color through socket/CLI surface APIs so agents and scripts can set/clear/read it without stealing macOS focus.
- Localize all user-facing strings.

## Out of scope

- Changing workspace color behavior or fixing workspace color chrome bugs beyond seams needed to coexist cleanly.
- Tinting terminal cells, browser pages, markdown content, or Ghostty internals.
- Adding full theme-token authoring for per-tab colors unless the implementation needs a narrow theme role for contrast.
- Making Lattice assign colors automatically; this ticket only provides the c11 primitive.

## Acceptance criteria

- A user can set, clear, and choose a custom color for an individual pane tab from the tab context menu.
- The selected tab and inactive tabs show the tab color in a legible, restrained way that does not obscure favicon/loading/pin/unread/dirty/shortcut states.
- Tab color follows the surface when tabs are reordered, moved to another pane, detached, or moved to another workspace/window.
- Tab color persists across session save/restore.
- CLI/socket support can set, clear, and list/read a surface tab color by surface ref.
- Workspace color and tab color can coexist: workspace color continues to drive workspace/sidebar/theme chrome; tab color only identifies the individual surface tab.
- All new UI strings are localized in `Resources/Localizable.xcstrings`.
- Tests cover color normalization/state mutation/persistence and Bonsplit tab model propagation through runtime seams, not source-text assertions.

## Validation

Use unit tests for pure state and snapshot behavior. Use a tagged c11 build for visual validation of terminal/browser/markdown tabs across light/dark themes, workspace custom colors, pinned tabs, unread badges, loading browser favicons, and narrow split panes.
title: Per-surface tab colors inside panes
